name: Galileon modified gravity
summary: Effective field theory of a scalar pi with derivative self-interactions invariant under an internal Galilean symmetry (pi -> pi + b_mu x^mu + c). The symmetry restricts the allowed terms to five 'Galileons' that yield non-linearities without ghost instabilities. Originated as the 4D effective theory of the DGP braneworld brane-bending mode; provides a consistent IR modification of gravity producing self-acceleration via Vainshtein-screened scalar dynamics.

addresses_puzzles.note: Galileon self-acceleration provides cosmic acceleration without a true Lambda; specific value not derived but the framework predicts a definite (and in some cases problematic) growth-of-structure history. Cubic Galileon is largely excluded by ISW, kinetic Sunyaev-Zel'dovich, and GW170817; quartic and quintic variants narrowly survive.

touches_benchmarks.note: Quartic and quintic Galileons in their original form predict c_GW != c; GW170817 substantially excluded these. Cubic Galileon is unaffected but is killed by ISW + cluster data.
Cubic Galileon predicts a stronger ISW signal than observed; combined Planck + ISW analyses exclude it (Renk et al. 2017).
Vainshtein screening recovers GR around dense sources; solar-system tests respected.

new_symmetries: Galilean symmetry pi -> pi + b_mu x^mu + c
introduces.note: Five Galileon coupling constants (one per term). The symmetry severely restricts the form.

history: Nicolis, Rattazzi, Trincherini (2008), 'The Galileon as a local modification of gravity', Phys. Rev. D 79, 064036, arXiv:0811.2197 — distilled the 4D EFT structure from DGP. Generalised Galileon / Horndeski (Deffayet-Esposito-Farese-Vikman 2009; Horndeski 1974 rediscovered). GW170817 (2017) ruled out the simplest derivative-tensor Horndeski models that predict c_GW != c.

references.citation: Nicolis, Rattazzi, Trincherini, 'The Galileon as a local modification of gravity', Phys. Rev. D 79, 064036 (2009)
